context: Tests vertical TDD slicing discipline: one test at a time, tracer bullet first, minimal implementation per cycle, no anticipating future tests. Also tests time-dependency injection for mockable clock control.

checklist.name: Tracer bullet documented
checklist.description: tdd-cycles.md shows the first cycle is the simplest possible test (e.g. 'first request is allowed') before more complex scenarios

checklist.name: Vertical slices only
checklist.description: tdd-cycles.md shows at least 5 distinct RED→GREEN cycles — not all tests written in one batch followed by one implementation

checklist.name: One test per cycle
checklist.description: Each cycle in tdd-cycles.md adds exactly one new test before implementation — not multiple tests at once

checklist.name: Minimal implementation per cycle
checklist.description: tdd-cycles.md shows implementation limited to making the current test pass — no features added ahead of tests

checklist.name: Time dependency injected
checklist.description: Rate limiter accepts a clock/time function as a parameter (e.g. getNow or clock) rather than calling Date.now() directly — so tests can control time

checklist.name: No real time delays in tests
checklist.description: Tests do NOT use setTimeout, sleep, or real time delays — they control time via the injected clock

checklist.name: Behavior-describing tests
checklist.description: Test names describe rate limiter behaviors (e.g. 'client is blocked after exceeding limit') not implementation (e.g. 'counter increments')

checklist.name: Public interface only
checklist.description: Tests only call public methods of the rate limiter — no access to internal counters, maps, or timestamps

checklist.name: No horizontal slicing
checklist.description: tdd-cycles.md does NOT show a phase of writing all tests followed by a phase of writing all implementation

checklist.name: Next test rationale given
checklist.description: tdd-cycles.md explains why each behavior was chosen as the next to test (sequencing rationale)
